item.title: Electric blues
item.description: Period1938–present (peak: late 1940s–late 1960s)LocationChicago, Memphis, Texas, Los AngelesInfluencesDelta bluesCountry bluesJump bluesGospelJazzLinksRYM A guitar string bent through a cheap amplifier in a loud club on Chicago’s South Side, the note hanging in distortion longer than any acoustic i...

item.title: Cultural omnivorousness
item.description: A philosophy professor who keeps a Dolly Parton record next to the Bach on the shelf would have puzzled the sociologist of 1970.

item.title: (1964) "You Really Got Me"
item.description: The KinksReleasedAugust 1964LabelPye RecordsProducerShel TalmyStudioIBC StudiosSongwritersRay DaviesGenresPrimaryGarage rockProto-punkSecondaryBritish rhythm & bluesHard rockPower popFreakbeatFrat rockListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Two power chords a whole step apart, hammered through a sound nob...

item.title: Christe — Sound of the Beast
item.description: Author: Ian Christe Title: Sound of the Beast: The Complete Headbanging History of Heavy Metal Year: 2003 Type: Book (HarperEntertainment) The dating is the thesis.

item.title: Dawes — What Are You Doing Here?
item.description: Author: Laina Dawes Title: What Are You Doing Here? A Black Woman’s Life and Liberation in Heavy Metal Year: 2012 Type: Book (Bazillion Points) The title is a question the author kept getting asked at shows.
